name: Ch01 日常生活 單元1-1

entries.sentence: Adam waved goodbye to his family and went aboard.
entries.translation: 亞當和家人揮手道別，上了船（飛機、車等）。

entries.sentence: When all the passengers are aboard the bus, it will leave.
entries.translation: 當所有乘客都上了巴士，它就會開走。

wordPlus.note: 以下單字字形相似，字義卻不同：

items.sentence: I plan to study abroad in Germany next year.
We can board the train at 3 p.m.
The strong man has broad shoulders.

entries.sentence: Signing up for our airline's membership program allows you to save up mileage and get discounts for your future trips.
entries.translation: 申請我們航空公司的會員可以讓你累積里程數，並在未來的旅程換取折扣。

entries.sentence: Alcohol can be used to kill bacteria.
entries.translation: 酒精可以用來殺菌。

entries.sentence: Beer and wine are different kinds of alcohol.
entries.translation: 啤酒和葡萄酒是不同的酒類。

entries.sentence: I get a weekly allowance of NT$500 from my parents.
entries.translation: 我父母每星期給我五百元臺幣的零用錢。

entries.sentence: Business people often get a travel allowance from their company to pay for their hotel and meals.
entries.translation: 業務員出差時，他們的公司通常會給他們食宿的差旅費。

entries.sentence: My sister loves to put nuts, like almonds, on top of her ice cream.
entries.translation: 我姐姐喜歡在冰淇淋上加杏仁之類的堅果。

entries.sentence: The law requires all cars to give way to any ambulance with its sirens on.
entries.translation: 法律規定所有車輛都須讓路給任何執行勤務中的救護車。

entries.sentence: Joe doesn't have enough money to buy an automobile, so he takes the bus to work.
entries.translation: 喬沒有足夠的錢買車子，所以只好搭公車上班。

entries.sentence: I like to eat bacon and eggs for breakfast.
entries.translation: 我早餐喜歡吃培根蛋。

phrases.sentence: When Tom hurt his back, Mary had to go to work to bring home the bacon.
phrases.translation: 湯姆背部受傷時，瑪麗得去工作以維持家計。

entries.sentence: Joan made a bargain with the carpenter—if he finished building the cabinets quickly, she'd give him more money.
entries.translation: 瓊安和木匠達成協議：如果他快點做完櫥櫃，她會多付他一些錢。

entries.chinese: 划算的交易；便宜貨 (= good deal)
entries.sentence: Buying the house at such a low price was a real bargain.
entries.translation: 用這麼低的價錢買到那間房子真是划算。

entries.sentence: We bargained with the fruit seller until he lowered his prices.
entries.translation: 我們跟水果商討價還價直到他算我們便宜點。

wordPlus.note: 下列名詞皆有「協議；約定」之意，惟語意用法略有不同：

items.chinese: 指有法律效力的正式契約、合同
items.sentence: You need to sign this contract before the bank gives you the loan.

items.chinese: 國家、公司等之間的書面或口頭協議、協定
items.sentence: The two companies reached an agreement on how to work together.
The presidents signed a peace treaty that ended the war.
Under the Geneva Convention, countries agree not to mistreat prisoners of war.

entries.sentence: This coffee is terribly bitter and needs some sugar to make it easy to drink.
entries.translation: 這杯咖啡實在太苦了，需要加入一些糖來使得它容易入口。

entries.sentence: I opened the door and was immediately struck by the bitter cold outside.
entries.translation: 我打開門，立刻被外面的酷寒嚇了一跳。

entries.chinese: 怨恨的；充滿仇恨的；無法釋懷的
entries.sentence: The bitter old man spent all of his time thinking about all the things that made him mad.
entries.translation: 那位滿懷仇恨的老人花全部的時間想著所有讓他憤怒的事物。

entries.sentence: Mary had trouble deciding which blouse went best with her new skirt.
entries.translation: 瑪麗很難決定哪件上衣最搭她的新裙子。

entries.sentence: Dr. Hill's bookcase is filled with medical books and patient records.
entries.translation: 希爾博士的書架上放滿了醫學書籍和患者的病歷。

entries.sentence: The man had to hit the brakes hard when a dog ran out in front of his car.
entries.translation: 一隻狗衝到那名男子的車前時，他只好緊急剎車。

entries.sentence: I fell on another passenger when the bus driver braked suddenly.
entries.translation: 公車司機突然剎車時，我跌倒在一名乘客身上。

phrases.sentence: The company put a brake on its project once the market weakened.
phrases.translation: 當市場疲弱不振，那家公司便暫停那項企畫案。

entries.sentence: Pamela won't have breakfast because she'll meet her friends for brunch at 11 a.m.
entries.translation: 潘美拉沒有要吃早餐，因為她會在11點和朋友碰面吃早午餐。

entries.sentence: If you want to try a lot of different foods, you can go to a buffet in a good hotel restaurant.
entries.translation: 如果你想品嚐許多不同的菜色，可以去高級飯店的餐廳吃自助餐。

wordPlus.note: 與「自助餐」相關的常見名詞區分如下：

items.chinese: 指吃到飽的自助式餐飲
指大學或辦公大樓內的自助餐

entries.sentence: Bill used to spend his holidays in a lovely cabin by a lake in northern Canada.
entries.translation: 比爾以前常在加拿大北方一座湖邊的美麗小木屋度假。

entries.sentence: Airline passengers aren't usually allowed to go into the pilot's cabin.
entries.translation: 飛機乘客通常不准進入駕駛艙內。

entries.sentence: Lily took some aspirin from her medicine cabinet to treat her headache.
entries.translation: 莉莉從藥櫃中拿了一些阿斯匹靈治頭痛。

entries.sentence: The prime minister will choose his new cabinet very soon.
entries.translation: 行政院長很快將選出新內閣成員。

entries.sentence: People in the 19th century got around by horse-drawn carriage.
entries.translation: 十九世紀的人以馬車代步。

entries.sentence: You should chew food well before swallowing it.
entries.translation: 食物在吞下前要細嚼。

entries.sentence: It is said that Santa Claus climbs down chimneys to deliver gifts on Christmas Eve.
entries.translation: 據說耶誕老人會在耶誕夜爬下煙囪送禮物。

entries.sentence: Tina often goes through her closet and picks out old clothes that she wants to give away to make room for new ones.
entries.translation: 為了騰出空間放新衣服，提娜經常翻查衣櫥，並選出她想要送人的舊衣服。

phrases.sentence: Almost every politician has a few skeletons in their closet, and the media often goes to great lengths to reveal these scandalous secrets.
phrases.translation: 幾乎每位政治人物都有不能說的秘密，而媒體時常竭盡全力揭發這些醜聞秘密。

phrases.sentence: Brad finally plucked up the courage and came out of the closet to his parents.
phrases.translation: 布萊德最後終於鼓起勇氣向他父母出櫃。

entries.sentence: Most cocktails are just a mix of different alcohols and are not hard to make.
entries.translation: 大部分的雞尾酒只是混合不同的酒類而已，而且並不會很難調。

entries.sentence: Bruce was almost killed when a coconut hit him on the head on a beach in Thailand.
entries.translation: 布魯斯在泰國的一處海灘時被掉下來的椰子擊中頭部，差點斃命。

entries.sentence: The collar of my shirt was too tight, so I found it hard to breathe.
entries.translation: 我襯衫的衣領太緊了，所以我覺得呼吸困難。

entries.sentence: The actors were all dressed in 16th-century costumes throughout the movie.
entries.translation: 那部電影裡的演員從頭到尾都穿著十六世紀的服裝。

wordPlus.note: 與「衣服」相關的常見名詞區分如下：

items.chinese: 服裝；衣著（比較正式、莊重的用語）
items.sentence: All of the garments at this clothing store are made by fair-trade companies.
Dora packed a few outfits and a bathing suit for her weekend trip.
The clothing store held a big summer sale, offering up to 50% off on select styles.
Despite Amy's large wardrobe, she complained she had nothing to wear to the party.

items.chinese: （常指特定樣式或正式的）服裝（不可數）
items.sentence: Isaac dressed in formal attire for the fancy black-tie event.

items.chinese: （常指特定種類的）服裝（常見於美式用法）
items.sentence: Before practicing for a marathon, you need to buy the proper sports apparel.

entries.sentence: We dream of buying a little cottage in the countryside to retire in.
entries.translation: 我們夢想在鄉間買一棟退休時住的小屋。

entries.sentence: The construction workers often use a crane to lift and move heavy things while putting up or tearing down a building.
entries.translation: 蓋房子或是拆除建築物的時候，這群建築工人常使用吊手來舉起或移動重物。

entries.sentence: A rare crane flew into Taiwan in 2016 and gained local fame, attracting hundreds of bird-watchers daily until the day it flew back home.
entries.translation: 一隻稀有的鶴在2016年飛到臺灣，並受到當地人歡迎，每天吸引上百位賞鳥客，直到牠飛回家。

entries.sentence: The small girl craned her neck to see the band onstage, but she could not see over the tall people.
entries.translation: 那位身材矮小的女孩伸長了她的脖子看舞臺上的樂團，但一群高個兒仍擋住她的視線。

entries.sentence: I like crispy toast, instead of soft bread, for my breakfast.
entries.translation: 我喜歡吃酥脆的烤吐司當早餐，不喜歡軟軟的麵包。

entries.sentence: Most kitchen cupboards contain at least some sugar, rice, flour, salt, and pepper.
entries.translation: 大部分的廚房櫥櫃裡至少都會有一些糖、米、麵粉、鹽和胡椒。

entries.sentence: The farm has a small dairy where it makes cheese.
entries.translation: 那座農場有一間製作乳酪的小型乳品廠。

entries.sentence: I can't eat dairy products such as yogurt and cheese.
entries.translation: 我不能吃像優格或起司之類的乳製品。

entries.sentence: The train departs from platform one at noon.
entries.translation: 火車在中午時從一號月臺開出。

entries.sentence: What is the departure time for the flight to Kaohsiung?
entries.translation: 飛往高雄的班機什麼時候起飛？

phrases.sentence: Seeing that there was no longer any need for his services, Fred decided to make his departure early.
phrases.translation: 既然不再需要他的服務，弗瑞德決定提早離開。

wordPlus.note: 字尾 -ure 為名詞字尾，接在動詞之後，表示「行為；結果；集合體」之意，如：

items.chinese: 接觸；宣傳；曝光（expose 揭露）
items.sentence: Henry's first exposure to rock music was as a young child when his dad would play it.
Taiwan's population is a mixture of indigenous tribes and people whose ancestors came from China.
The legislature is currently debating whether drones should be further regulated.

entries.sentence: Cindy and James went to a fancy restaurant last week that included a three-course meal and ended with an elegant dessert created by the chef.
entries.translation: 辛蒂和詹姆士上週去了一間高級餐廳，共有三道菜，最後是主廚自創的雅緻甜點。

entries.sentence: I don't feel like cooking; let's dine out tonight.
entries.translation: 我不想下廚，今晚我們出去吃吧！

entries.sentence: As a child, Nora liked to sit on the docks and watch the boats.
entries.translation: 諾拉小時候喜歡坐在碼頭上看來往的船隻。
